def solve(nums, p, queries)
    
    # Helper function for GCD
    def gcd(a, b)
        a = a.abs
        b = b.abs
        while b != 0
            a, b = b, a % b
        end
        a
    end

    N = nums.length
    count = 0

    # Helper function to check existence of a good subsequence
    def check_existence(nums, p, N)
        # 1. Construct A' (elements divisible by p, divided by p)
        s_prime = []
        for i in 0...N
            if nums[i] % p == 0
                s_prime << nums[i] / p
            end
        end

        n_active = s_prime.length

        if n_active == 0
            return false
        end

        # 2. Calculate g = GCD(A')
        g = s_prime[0]
        for i in 1...n_active
            g = gcd(g, s_prime[i])
        end

        # If GCD > 1, no subsequence can have GCD = 1 (and thus GCD = p).
        if g > 1
            return false 
        end

        # g == 1. Check length constraint.
        
        if n_active < N
            # Case 1: N_active < N. A' itself is a proper subsequence of length N_active < N, and GCD(A') = 1.
            return true
        else 
            # N_active == N. All elements are divisible by p. nums' = S_prime.
            # We need a proper subsequence S' subset of nums' such that GCD(S')=1.
            
            if N >= 3
                # Case 2a: If GCD(nums') = 1 and N >= 3, a proper subsequence with GCD 1 always exists.
                return true
            elsif N == 2
                # Case 2b: N = 2. nums' = [a, b]. GCD(a, b) = 1.
                # Proper subsequences are {a} and {b}. For GCD=1, we need a=1 or b=1.
                return s_prime[0] == 1 || s_prime[1] == 1
            end 
            # N < 2 is excluded by constraints (N >= 2).
            return false 
        end
    end

    for query in queries
        ind, val = query
        
        # Perform update
        nums[ind] = val
        
        # Check condition after update
        if check_existence(nums, p, N)
            count += 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

def solve(nums, p, queries)
    
    # Standard Euclidean algorithm for GCD
    def gcd(a, b)
        a = a.abs
        b = b.abs
        while b != 0
            a, b = b, a % b
        end
        a
    end

    n = nums.length
    count = 0

    # Helper function to check existence of a good subsequence
    def check_existence(nums, p, n)
        # 1. Filter elements divisible by p and scale them (S')
        s_prime = []
        for i in 0...n
            if nums[i] % p == 0
                s_prime << nums[i] / p
            end
        end

        n_active = s_prime.length

        if n_active == 0
            return false
        end

        # 2. Calculate g = GCD(S')
        g = s_prime[0]
        (1...n_active).each do |i|
            g = gcd(g, s_prime[i])
        end

        # If GCD > 1, no subsequence can have GCD = 1 (and thus GCD = p).
        if g > 1
            return false 
        end

        # g == 1. Check length constraint: we need a subsequence S' of length < n.
        
        if n_active < n
            # Case 1: At least one element is not divisible by p. 
            # S' itself is a proper subsequence of length n_active < n, and GCD(S') = 1.
            return true
        else 
            # n_active == n. All elements are divisible by p. nums' = s_prime.
            # We need a proper subsequence S' subset of nums' such that GCD(S')=1.
            
            if n >= 3
                # Case 2a: If GCD(nums') = 1 and N >= 3, a proper subsequence with GCD 1 always exists.
                return true
            elsif n == 2
                # Case 2b: N = 2. nums' = [a, b]. GCD(a, b) = 1.
                # Proper subsequences are {a} and {b}. For GCD=1, we need a=1 or b=1.
                return s_prime[0] == 1 || s_prime[1] == 1
            end 
            # N < 2 is excluded by constraints (N >= 2).
            return false 
        end
    end


    # Process queries
    queries.each do |query|
        ind = query[0]
        val = query[1]
        
        # Perform update
        nums[ind] = val
        
        # Check condition after update
        if check_existence(nums, p, n)
            count += 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

def solve(nums, p, queries)
    # Standard Euclidean algorithm for GCD
    def gcd(a, b)
        a = Math.abs(a)
        b = Math.abs(b)
        while b != 0
            a, b = b, a % b
        end
        return a
    end

    n = nums.length
    count = 0

    # Helper function to check existence of a good subsequence
    check_existence = Proc.new do
        s_prime = []
        for i in 0...n
            if nums[i] % p == 0
                s_prime << nums[i] / p
            end
        end

        n_active = s_prime.length

        if n_active == 0
            return false
        end

        # Calculate g = GCD(S')
        g = s_prime[0]
        for i in 1...n_active
            g = gcd(g, s_prime[i])
        end

        # If GCD > 1, no subsequence can have GCD = 1 (and thus GCD = p).
        if g > 1
            return false
        end

        # g == 1. Check length constraint: we need a subsequence S' of length < N.
        
        if n_active < n
            # Case 1: At least one element is not divisible by p. 
            # S' itself is a proper subsequence of length N_active < N, and GCD(S') = 1.
            return true
        else 
            # N_active == N. All elements are divisible by p. nums' = S_prime.
            # We need a proper subsequence S' subset of nums' such that GCD(S')=1.
            
            if n >= 3
                # Case 2a: If GCD(nums') = 1 and N >= 3, a proper subsequence with GCD 1 always exists.
                return true
            elsif n == 2
                # Case 2b: N = 2. nums' = [a, b]. GCD(a, b) = 1.
                # Proper subsequences are {a} and {b}. For GCD=1, we need a=1 or b=1.
                return s_prime[0] == 1 || s_prime[1] == 1
            end 
            # N < 2 is excluded by constraints (N >= 2).
            return false 
        end
    end


    for query in queries
        ind = query[0]
        val = query[1]
        
        # Perform update
        nums[ind] = val
        
        # Check condition after update
        if check_existence.call
            count += 1
        end
    end

    return count
end

    while (b) {
        [a, b] = [b, a % b];
    }
    return Math.abs(a);
}

function solve(nums, p, queries) {
    const N = nums.length;
    let count = 0;

    // Helper function to check existence of a good subsequence

        for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {

                S_prime.push(nums[i] / p);
            }

        const N_active = S_prime.length;

        if (N_active === 0) {

        }

        // Calculate g = GCD(S')
        let g = S_prime[0];
        for (let i = 1; i < N_active; i++) {
            g = gcd(g, S_prime[i]);
        }

        if (g > 1) {

        }

        // g == 1. Check length constraint.

        } else {

                // Requires a=1 or b=1.
                return S_prime[0] === 1 || S_prime[1] === 1;

        // Check condition after update
        if (checkExistence()) {

        }
    }

    return count;
}
